source_paper_title: Upper Bounds for All and Max-Gain Policy Iteration Algorithms on Deterministic MDPs

Section 6 (Summary and outlook), page 31.

It remains unknown if such a Hamiltonian path through the set of policies exists for any MDP with \(k \ge 3\) actions.
title: Construct Hamiltonian policy-iteration paths for multi-action Markov decision processes
background_latex:
Consider a finite discounted Markov decision process (MDP) \(M=(S,A,T,R,\gamma)\) with finite state set \(S\) and action set \(A\), transition probabilities \(T:S\times A\times S\to[0,1]\) (so \(\sum_{s'\in S}T(s,a,s')=1\)), reward function \(R:S\times A\to\mathbb{R}\), and discount factor \(\gamma\in[0,1)\). A (deterministic, stationary/Markovian) policy is a map \(\pi:S\to A\). For a policy \(\pi\), its value function \(V^\pi:S\to\mathbb{R}\) is
\[
V^\pi(s)=\mathbb{E}\Big[\sum_{t=0}^{\infty}\gamma^t R(s_t,a_t)\Big],\quad s_0=s,\ a_t=\pi(s_t),\ s_{t+1}\sim T(s_t,a_t,\cdot).
\]
For policies \(\pi,\pi'\), write \(\pi\prec\pi'\) if \(V^\pi(s)\le V^{\pi'}(s)\) for all \(s\in S\) and \(V^\pi(s)<V^{\pi'}(s)\) for some \(s\). For a policy \(\pi\), define the action-value function \(Q^\pi(s,a)=R(s,a)+\gamma\sum_{s'\in S}T(s,a,s')V^\pi(s')\) and the gain \(\rho^\pi(s,a)=Q^\pi(s,a)-V^\pi(s)\). A policy improvement step replaces \(\pi\) by a policy \(\pi'\) that differs from \(\pi\) on a nonempty set of states, switching each such state \(s\) to an action \(a\) with \(\rho^\pi(s,a)>0\); the policy improvement theorem guarantees \(\pi\prec\pi'\).

Define the policy-improvement directed acyclic graph (PI-DAG) of \(M\) as the directed graph whose vertices are all policies \(\pi:S\to A\), with a directed edge \(\pi\to\pi'\) whenever \(\pi'\) can be obtained from \(\pi\) by one policy improvement step (switching any nonempty set of improving actions). A directed path in the PI-DAG corresponds to a possible trajectory of some policy-iteration (PI) algorithm on \(M\). A Hamiltonian path in the PI-DAG is a directed path that visits every policy exactly once; for \(|S|=n\) and \(|A|=k\), this would have length \(k^n\) (visiting all \(k^n\) policies).
problem_definition_latex: For integers \(n\ge 1\) and \(k\ge 3\), determine whether there exists a discounted MDP \(M=(S,A,T,R,\gamma)\) with \(|S|=n\) and \(|A|=k\) whose PI-DAG contains a Hamiltonian directed path, i.e., a directed path that visits all \(k^n\) deterministic stationary policies exactly once via successive policy-improvement steps.
latex:
Consider a finite discounted Markov decision process (MDP) \(M=(S,A,T,R,\gamma)\) with finite state set \(S\) and action set \(A\), transition probabilities \(T:S\times A\times S\to[0,1]\) (so \(\sum_{s'\in S}T(s,a,s')=1\)), reward function \(R:S\times A\to\mathbb{R}\), and discount factor \(\gamma\in[0,1)\). A (deterministic, stationary/Markovian) policy is a map \(\pi:S\to A\). For a policy \(\pi\), its value function \(V^\pi:S\to\mathbb{R}\) is
\[
V^\pi(s)=\mathbb{E}\Big[\sum_{t=0}^{\infty}\gamma^t R(s_t,a_t)\Big],\quad s_0=s,\ a_t=\pi(s_t),\ s_{t+1}\sim T(s_t,a_t,\cdot).
\]
For policies \(\pi,\pi'\), write \(\pi\prec\pi'\) if \(V^\pi(s)\le V^{\pi'}(s)\) for all \(s\in S\) and \(V^\pi(s)<V^{\pi'}(s)\) for some \(s\). For a policy \(\pi\), define the action-value function \(Q^\pi(s,a)=R(s,a)+\gamma\sum_{s'\in S}T(s,a,s')V^\pi(s')\) and the gain \(\rho^\pi(s,a)=Q^\pi(s,a)-V^\pi(s)\). A policy improvement step replaces \(\pi\) by a policy \(\pi'\) that differs from \(\pi\) on a nonempty set of states, switching each such state \(s\) to an action \(a\) with \(\rho^\pi(s,a)>0\); the policy improvement theorem guarantees \(\pi\prec\pi'\).

Define the policy-improvement directed acyclic graph (PI-DAG) of \(M\) as the directed graph whose vertices are all policies \(\pi:S\to A\), with a directed edge \(\pi\to\pi'\) whenever \(\pi'\) can be obtained from \(\pi\) by one policy improvement step (switching any nonempty set of improving actions). A directed path in the PI-DAG corresponds to a possible trajectory of some policy-iteration (PI) algorithm on \(M\). A Hamiltonian path in the PI-DAG is a directed path that visits every policy exactly once; for \(|S|=n\) and \(|A|=k\), this would have length \(k^n\) (visiting all \(k^n\) policies).

For integers \(n\ge 1\) and \(k\ge 3\), determine whether there exists a discounted MDP \(M=(S,A,T,R,\gamma)\) with \(|S|=n\) and \(|A|=k\) whose PI-DAG contains a Hamiltonian directed path, i.e., a directed path that visits all \(k^n\) deterministic stationary policies exactly once via successive policy-improvement steps.
open_problem_quote: It remains unknown if such a Hamiltonian path through the set of policies exists for any MDP with \(k \ge 3\) actions.
counterexample_suitability_rationale: For fixed small \(n,k\) (e.g., \(n=3,k=3\)), one can computationally search for an MDP whose PI-DAG contains a Hamiltonian path by parameterizing \(T,R,\gamma\) and checking all \(k^n\) policies and improvement edges numerically. The search space is large but finite-dimensional, and feasibility of a candidate can be tested by solving Bellman equations and verifying gains.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: Candidate MDP instances can be represented by real-valued parameters \((T,R,\gamma)\) (or restricted families thereof), and a fitness signal can be the length of the longest policy-improvement trajectory achievable (or presence/absence of a Hamiltonian path) computed from gains across all policies. An evolutionary loop can iteratively mutate parameters to increase achievable path length and potentially discover explicit small-instance constructions.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.

literature_review.synthesis:
The open problem asks whether, for \(n\ge 1\) and \(k\ge 3\), there exists a discounted \(n\)-state \(k\)-action MDP whose policy-improvement DAG (PI-DAG) contains a Hamiltonian directed path, i.e., a policy-iteration trajectory that visits all \(k^n\) deterministic stationary policies exactly once via successive policy-improvement steps. The source paper establishes that such Hamiltonian behavior is possible in the 2-state setting for every \(k\ge 2\) (Proposition 2), by explicitly constructing a 2-state \(k\)-action discounted MDP and a switching rule that forces PI to traverse all \(k^2\) policies. This generalizes the earlier 2-action Hamiltonian construction of Melekopoglou and Condon to multi-action MDPs, but only for \(n=2\).

For \(n\ge 3\) and \(k\ge 3\), no Hamiltonian construction is currently known from the provided material. The strongest general lower bounds on PI trajectory length for multi-action MDPs remain far from \(k^n\): Ashutosh et al. give instances with paths of length \(\Omega(k^{n/2})\). On the upper-bound side, a substantial literature (e.g., Mansour--Singh and Scherrer, and later improvements such as Gupta--Kalyanakrishnan) studies how switching rules constrain the maximum possible number of improvements, but these do not resolve whether the PI-DAG of some MDP can realize a full Hamiltonian path when \(k\ge 3\).

A notable contrast is provided by deterministic transitions (DMDPs): Post--Ye show strong polynomiality for a max-gain simplex variant, and the source paper proves exponential-but-sub-\(k^n\) upper bounds for all PI variants on DMDPs using a graph-theoretic analysis of cycles/path-cycles. These results suggest that any Hamiltonian phenomenon for \(k\ge 3\) must exploit stochasticity in transitions, as already seen in the 2-state construction. The main gap is therefore to either (i) build an explicit \(n\ge 3\), \(k\ge 3\) stochastic MDP whose PI-DAG admits a Hamiltonian path (perhaps starting with \(n=k=3\)), or (ii) prove a structural obstruction showing that Hamiltonian PI paths cannot exist once \(k\ge 3\) and \(n\ge 3\).

sources.title: Upper Bounds for All and Max-Gain Policy Iteration Algorithms on Deterministic MDPs
sources.theorem_or_result:
Formulates the PI-DAG viewpoint and poses the open question of whether, for 
\(n\ge 1\) and \(k\ge 3\), there exists a discounted \(n\)-state \(k\)-action MDP whose PI-DAG contains a Hamiltonian directed path visiting all \(k^n\) deterministic stationary policies exactly once via policy-improvement steps. Proves existence for \(n=2\) and all \(k\ge 2\) (Proposition 2), and gives upper bounds for deterministic MDPs.
sources.connection: This is the source paper that states the open problem and provides the only known Hamiltonian construction in the 2-state case; it motivates the extension to \(n\ge 3\), \(k\ge 3\).

sources.title: On the complexity of the policy improvement algorithm for Markov decision processes
sources.theorem_or_result: Constructs an \(n\)-state, 2-action discounted MDP and a policy-iteration switching rule whose trajectory visits all \(2^n\) policies, i.e., a Hamiltonian path exists in the PI-DAG for \(k=2\).
sources.connection: Shows Hamiltonian PI paths are possible in the binary-action case, motivating the open question for \(k\ge 3\).

sources.title: Lower bounds for policy iteration on multi-action MDPs
sources.theorem_or_result: Gives exponential lower bounds for policy iteration on multi-action MDPs, constructing instances whose PI-DAG contains directed paths of length \(\Omega(k^{n/2})\).
sources.connection: Provides the best-known general lower bound for \(k\ge 3\), far from Hamiltonian length \(k^n\), and highlights the gap the open problem targets.

sources.title: On the complexity of policy iteration
sources.theorem_or_result: Provides upper bounds on iterations for variants of Howard's policy iteration and randomized switching rules, including bounds of order \(O(k^n/n)\) for Howard's PI with arbitrary action selection.
sources.connection: Gives baseline worst-case upper bounds for PI trajectories in general MDPs, framing how extreme Hamiltonian-length paths would be.

sources.title: The simplex method is strongly polynomial for deterministic Markov decision processes
sources.theorem_or_result: Shows the max-gain simplex (single-switch PI) rule is strongly polynomial on deterministic MDPs by analyzing cycle creation/destruction in the induced graph.
sources.connection: While about deterministic transitions (DMDPs), it develops cycle-based structural tools that influence later PI-DAG/path analyses and contrasts with the stochastic MDP Hamiltonian-path question.

sources.title: Improved and generalized upper bounds on the complexity of policy iteration
sources.theorem_or_result: Derives improved upper bounds on the number of iterations of policy iteration under various switching rules, extending earlier analyses and relating PI to simplex-like pivot rules.
sources.connection: Provides general upper-bound techniques and context for how long PI trajectories can be, relevant when comparing to potential Hamiltonian-length constructions.

sources.title: Improved strong worst-case upper bounds for MDP planning
sources.theorem_or_result: Gives improved worst-case upper bounds (in terms of \(n,k\)) for certain deterministic PI variants for MDP planning, using batch-switching ideas.
sources.connection: Represents progress on bounding PI trajectory lengths; Hamiltonian PI paths would match the trivial \(k^n\) bound, so improved upper bounds constrain which variants could realize Hamiltonian paths.
